title: Bypass safety matrix consistency suite: README.md and bee-bypass-gate SKILL.md matrices parsed and compared, drift fails verify
action: Per D6: README.md (:192-197) and skills/bee-bypass-gate/SKILL.md (:27-31) each hand-maintain an off/normal/full/total bypass matrix with no guard against drift — the exact 'two sources of truth' seam #54 warns about. Write scripts/test_bypass_matrix.mjs: parse both markdown tables (tolerant of prose/formatting differences — normalize each level row to a semantic tuple: which gates auto-approve, whether high-risk/hard-gate is covered, whether secret reads stop, whether Gate 4/P1 stops), assert the two tables agree level-by-level, and fail with a message naming the first divergent level and both readings. Also assert all four levels exist in both files. PLAN-CHECK W2: NO manual registration — run_verify.mjs auto-discovers scripts/test_*.mjs via DISCOVERY_ROOTS glob (:42-47, :315-334); adding an EXTRA_SUITES entry would DOUBLE-RUN the suite; just confirm node scripts/run_verify.mjs --only test_bypass_matrix finds it. No runtime code changes; no README/SKILL.md content changes unless the parse reveals an ACTUAL semantic divergence — if it does, STOP and return [BLOCKED] with both readings instead of silently editing either doc.

truths: the suite fails on semantic drift between the two matrices
prohibitions: do not edit README or SKILL.md content in this cell

trace.outcome: Wrote scripts/test_bypass_matrix.mjs: parses README.md's per-column bypass matrix and skills/bee-bypass-gate/SKILL.md's combined-prose matrix into a shared {gates13,highRisk,gate4P1,secret} semantic tuple per level (off/normal/full/total), diffs level-by-level, fails naming the first divergent level+flag+both raw rows. Checked: no actual semantic divergence exists between the two matrices today (all 4 levels agree) — nothing to BLOCK on. Auto-discovered by run_verify.mjs (no manual registration, confirmed via --only). Includes sentinel checks proving the differ both catches genuine drift and tolerates cosmetic-only formatting differences.
